26.reason: The arguments to memset are in the wrong order; it should be memset(cnt, 1, sizeof(cnt)) to initialize all elements to 1.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  The arguments to memset are in the wrong order; it should be memset(cnt, 1, sizeof(cnt)) to initialize all elements to 1.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: The arguments to memset are in the wrong order. It should be memset(cnt, 1, sizeof(cnt)); The current call sets only the first byte of cnt to 1, not all elements.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Wrong arguments: memset expects (pointer, value, size in bytes). Here it sets only the first sizeof(int) bytes to 1, leaving most of cnt uninitialized. Should be memset(cnt, 1, sizeof(cnt)) to initialize all elements to 1.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Arguments to memset are swapped; it should be memset(cnt, 0, sizeof(cnt)) or similar, and initializing cnt to 1 requires a loop.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error] The arguments for memset are in the wrong order (should be pointer, value, size). Additionally, memset operates on bytes, so it cannot correctly initialize an int array to 1. A loop should be used instead.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Arguments to memset are in wrong order; should be memset(cnt, 0, sizeof(cnt)) or similar. Current call sets only first sizeof(int) bytes to 1, and doesn't initialize all elements properly.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Arguments to memset are in wrong order; should be memset(cnt, 0, sizeof(cnt)) or similar. Current call sets only 4 bytes (sizeof(int)) to value 1, and doesn't initialize all elements properly.]

32.reason: Missing '&' before each variable; scanf requires pointers to store values.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing address-of operator & before each variable; should be &l[i].p1.x, etc.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: Missing address-of operator (&) before each variable. Should be: scanf("%d%d%d%d", &l[i].p1.x, &l[i].p1.y, &l[i].p2.x, &l[i].p2.y);
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Missing address-of operator & before each variable; scanf expects pointers, not ints. This causes undefined behavior.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Missing '&' before each variable; scanf requires pointers to store values.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Missing '&' operator for scanf arguments, which expect pointers to int, causing a compilation error.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error] scanf requires pointers to the variables to store the input values. The '&' operator is missing before each variable, causing a type mismatch compilation error.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Missing & operator before each variable in scanf; should be &l[i].p1.x etc.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Missing & operator before each variable in scanf; should be &l[i].p1.x etc. This causes undefined behavior as scanf expects pointers.]
